items.title: The test pyramid — where unit tests sit
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB E2E["E2E tests
(seconds–minutes each, few)"] INT["Integration tests
(hundreds of ms each, moderate)"] UNIT["Unit tests
(milliseconds each, many)"] E2E --- INT --- UNIT style UNIT fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a style INT fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04 style E2E fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626
items.caption: The pyramid: many fast unit tests at the base, fewer integration tests, very few slow E2E tests at the top.

items.title: The AAA pattern visualised
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR A["Arrange
set up inputs, mocks, fixtures"] --> B["Act
call the function under test"] --> C["Assert
check outcome / state / calls"]
items.caption: Every unit test has these three phases — even when one phase is a single line.
